title: Spicy Vietnamese Rest-Meat Banh Cuon
description: A spicy and comforting Vietnamese dish featuring rest-meat and crunchy rice noodles, suitable for no-onion-no-garlic diet.

ingredients.name: Rest-meat
ingredients.notes: Use store-bought rest-meat or substitute with cooked chicken.

instructions: Soak the rice noodles in hot water for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Cut the rest-meat into thin strips.
Heat the vegetable oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add the rest-meat and cook until browned, about 3 minutes.
Add the bean sprouts, shredded carrot, and chopped fresh mint and basil to the pan, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the steamed rice noodles to the pan, and toss everything together to combine.
Season with fish sauce, soy sauce, and grated ginger.
Add the ground black pepper and salt to taste.
Transfer the Banh Cuon to a serving plate, and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Serve with spicy sauce on the side, if desired.
For the spicy sauce, combine 2 tablespoons of hot water with 2 tablespoons of spicy sauce in a small bowl, and mix well.
Heat the mixture over low heat, and stir constantly until the sauce is warm and fragrant.
Serve the spicy sauce on the side, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
